LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, MAR-05-2021 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANT SHAIL TALATI'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT SHAIL TALATI'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR. CCP 430.41 REQUIRES A "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" BEFORE A DEMURRER MAY BE FILED; COUNSEL'S DECLARATION INDICATES THAT NO SUCH MEET AND CONFER OCCURRED. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY AND DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY APRIL 2, 2021. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SHAIL TALATI, TECHIFY SOLUTIONS PVT. LTD. DBA X2SKY'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT'S (FAC) THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION IS SUSTAINED. THE DEMURRER TO THE FAC'S FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANTS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND THAT IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. (SEE THE TENTATIVE RULING OF THE MINI-MINUTES FOR THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ROSE MILLER, CSR NO. 5041, EMAIL: RMILLERCSR@GMAIL.COM. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-07-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
KIMON CAMBOUROGLOU, Esq., (415) 889-4230, kimon@technologylawgroup.net, for Plaintiff, RANSOM LAB INC.,
Raquel E. Howard, Esq., CLARK HILL LLP, (619) 819-2444, rhoward@clarkhill.com , for Defendants, TECHIFY SOLUTIONS PYT. LTD. DBA X2SKY; SHAIL TALATI.
Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, adopts the tentative ruling as follows:(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

PLAINTIFF RANSOM LAB INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT.

"Plaintiff Ransom Lab Inc.'s demurrer to defendants' cross complaint" (CC) is sustained in part and overruled in part.

Ransom's two grounds for demurring are: "(a) the CC's Fifth COA for Negligent and / or Intentional Misrepresentation does not allege duty or harm and (b) the CC's Sixth COA for fraud does not meet the heightened pleading standard."
(a) Duty is not a requisite element of negligent or intentional misrepresentation (CACI 1900, 1903), but it is nonetheless pled (CC 7:21-24). Harm is also adequately pled. (Id. at 7:17-20, 8:21-24.) Thus, demurrer (a) is overruled.
(b) A fraud pleading must state "how, when, where, to whom, and by what means" the misrepresentations were made, which the cross-complaint fails to do. (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) Thus, demurrer (b) is sustained with leave to amend.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-25-2021 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS SHAIL TALATI AND TECHIFY SOLUTIONS PVT. LTD. DBA X2SKY'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' demurrer to the fourth cause of action of the second amended complaint is overruled. The Court previously sustained defendants' demurrer to this cause of action with leave to amend on the ground that it did not plead fraud with the required specificity. (Lazar v. Superior Court (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) The second amended complaint adequately complies with the particularity requirement, as it specifically alleges who made the representations, to whom, and when. (See, e.g., SAC pars. 7, 8, 10, 14, 53-54.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-12-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS SHAIL TALATI, TECHIFY SOLUTIONS PVT. LTD. DBA X2SKY'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendants' demurrer to the first amended complaint's (FAC) third cause of action for negligent misrepresentation is sustained. This new cause of action - not authorized by my prior demurrer order - requires a motion for leave to amend. (CCP 472.)

The demurrer to the FAC's fourth cause of action for fraud is sustained with leave to amend. The FAC still does not plead with the required specificity - "how, when, where, to whom, and by what means." (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.)

Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

CCP 430.41 requires a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a demurrer may be filed; counsel's declaration indicates that no such meet and confer occurred. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and defendant's response to the amended complaint is now due by April 2, 2021.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-19-2020 Continued To Jan-20-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-04-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. KIMON CAMBOUROGLOU, Esq. of Ransom Lab Inc. (Tel: 415-889-4230) for Plaintiff
Ransom Lab Inc.;
Atty. RAQUEL E. HOWARD, Esq. of Clark Hill LLP (Tel: 619-819-2444) for Defendant Shail Talati.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SHAIL TALATI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Shail Talati's demurrers are sustained in part and overruled in part.

A complaint "is adequate so long as it apprises the defendant of the factual basis for the plaintiff's claim." (McKell v. Washington Mutual, Inc. (2006) 142 Cal.App.4th 1457, 1470.) By this standard, the complaint adequately pleads negligence against Talati. (Id. at 9:3-16.) Whether Talati actually had a duty to plaintiff is not for resolution at the pleading stage. However, the complaint fails to plead fraud with the required particularity. Thus, the demurrers are sustained with leave to amend as to the fraud count and otherwise overruled.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
